description: Paginated index of VGA (Visual GUI Automation) runs. Shows one row per row of runner.vga_runs joined to runner.vga_state_machines on state_machine_id. Columns: truncated run id linking to /vga/runs/[runId], state machine name, target process, status badge, started-at timestamp, duration. Supports filtering by status, target_process (exact match), and started_at date range (since/until). Filter state is mirrored into the URL query string so a reload or shared link restores the view. Pagination: 50 rows/page; total row count is computed server-side via COUNT(*) OVER() in the same query as the page slice. This page is the user's window into the Python VGA worker's execution history — the canonical place to see which runs succeeded, failed, drifted, or are still running for a given app.

groups.name: Page Structure & Layout
groups.description: Top-level layout: a sticky header with back-to-/vga link, heading, and total badge; a scrollable main area with a filters card followed by the runs table card. The viewport height is clamped to `calc(100vh-44px)` so the inner main section scrolls independently of the app shell.

assertions.description: A page header is rendered with the VGA runs heading. This anchors the page and provides the back-navigation affordance.
A 'Back to VGA landing' link is present in the header that routes to /vga. The icon is an ArrowLeft from lucide-react; the accessible name comes from the aria-label on the surrounding <Link>.
Clicking the back link navigates to /vga (the VGA landing page listing state machines). The URL should change to /vga and the 'VGA state machines' heading should render.
A badge displays the total run count in the header, formatted as '<N> total' where N is the COUNT(*) OVER() from the API. When no runs exist it shows '0 total'.
The main area renders exactly two Card containers: the Filters card first, then the Runs table card. The filters card is always above the table.

groups.name: Filters Card
groups.description: The filters card exposes four filters: status (Select), target_process (free-text Input with exact-match semantics), since (date Input), until (date Input), plus a Reset button. Changing any filter resets page to 1 and triggers a fresh /api/vga/runs query.

assertions.description: A Status select trigger with id='filter-status' is rendered. Its options are: All, Running, Succeeded, Failed, Drifted. The default value is 'All' (internal value 'all'), which sends no status param to the API.

Selecting a status (Running, Succeeded, Failed, or Drifted) adds `status=<value>` to the URL query string and triggers a GET /api/vga/runs?status=<value>... The backend rejects any status not in {running, succeeded, failed, drifted} with a 400, so the select must never surface a value outside that set.

A target-process input with id='filter-target' is rendered. Placeholder is 'notepad++.exe' to hint at OS-level process names. Matching is exact on sm.target_process (an indexed column on runner.vga_state_machines).

Typing into the target-process input updates the controlled state on every keystroke; the URL + API only refresh once the user blurs the input or presses Enter (onBlur/onKeyDown='Enter' both call resetToFirstPage). This avoids one request per character.
A date input with id='filter-since' is rendered. Value format: YYYY-MM-DD. The client expands bare dates to start-of-day UTC ('T00:00:00Z') before calling the API.
A date input with id='filter-until' is rendered. Value format: YYYY-MM-DD. The client expands bare dates to end-of-day UTC ('T23:59:59Z') before calling the API so the selected day is inclusive.

A 'Reset' button is rendered at the end of the filter row. Clicking it resets status to 'all', target_process to '', since to '', until to '', and page to 1 — stripping the URL back to /vga/runs with no query string.
Clicking Reset removes all filter query params from the URL (URL becomes /vga/runs with no '?'), clears each filter input's visible value, and re-runs the API query with default limit=50&offset=0.

groups.name: Runs Table
groups.description: When runs exist, a 6-column table renders the current page of results: Run id (truncated to first 8 chars, linked to /vga/runs/[runId]), State machine name, Target process, Status badge, Started-at (locale datetime), Duration. Rows are ordered by started_at DESC.

assertions.description: When runs are present, the table renders exactly these headers in order: Run, State machine, Target process, Status, Started, Duration. These correspond to the 6 TableHead cells in the source.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (data.runs.length > 0)

assertions.description: The number of data rows (tbody > tr) equals min(PAGE_SIZE=50, total - offset). On page N, rows represent indices [(N-1)*50, min(N*50, total)).
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: Each row's first cell is a monospace Link to /vga/runs/{run.id}, rendering only the first 8 characters of the run UUID as its visible text. Clicking navigates to the run-detail page.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: If a row's joined state_machine_name is null (orphaned run — SM deleted), the cell falls back to rendering the first 8 chars of run.stateMachineId in a muted monospace style instead of showing 'null' or an empty cell.

target.label: Run row with missing state machine

assertions.precondition: At least one run exists whose state machine has been deleted

assertions.description: If row.targetProcess is null, the target-process cell renders an em-dash ('—') in muted color rather than an empty cell.
assertions.precondition: At least one run exists whose state machine row has target_process = NULL

assertions.description: The Started column renders new Date(startedAt).toLocaleString() — a human-readable datetime in the viewer's locale, not the raw ISO string. This column uses a muted text tone to de-emphasize the metadata.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: The Duration column uses formatDuration(startedAt, endedAt): null endedAt → '—'; <1000ms → '<N>ms'; <60s → '<N.N>s'; <1h → '<M>m <S>s'; otherwise '<H>h <M>m'. Duration is computed client-side from the raw timestamps; it is not stored on the row.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

groups.name: Run Status Badges
groups.description: Each row's Status cell renders a Badge whose variant encodes status. The statusTone helper maps: succeeded|success→secondary, failed→destructive, drifted→outline, running→default, any other→outline. The label shown capitalizes the first letter except for unknown statuses which pass through raw.

assertions.description: Rows with status='succeeded' (or 'success' from legacy data) render a secondary-variant badge labeled 'Succeeded'. The secondary variant uses a subdued background — it is NOT the destructive red — so succeeded runs are visually distinct from failures.
assertions.precondition: At least one run with status in {succeeded, success} is visible on the current page

assertions.description: Rows with status='failed' render a destructive-variant badge labeled 'Failed', using the theme's destructive color token. This is the only status that uses the destructive variant.
assertions.precondition: At least one run with status='failed' is visible on the current page

assertions.description: Rows with status='drifted' render an outline-variant badge labeled 'Drifted'. 'Drifted' is a VGA-specific status meaning the grounding model produced a different bbox than stored — the run finished but validation flagged a pixel-level mismatch.
assertions.precondition: At least one run with status='drifted' is visible on the current page

assertions.description: Rows with status='running' render a default-variant badge labeled 'Running'. This is the only in-progress state; endedAt is null for these rows and the Duration column shows '—'.
assertions.precondition: At least one run with status='running' is visible on the current page

assertions.description: Every status badge must have sufficient contrast between its text color and fill color to remain legible in both light and dark mode. Badges use theme tokens (secondary/destructive/outline/default) that define foreground+background pairs together, so any mismatch is a theme regression rather than per-badge styling.
assertions.precondition: At least one status badge is visible

groups.name: Loading / Empty / Error Lifecycle
groups.description: The runs card renders one of four mutually exclusive sub-states based on the React Query status: isLoading → spinner, error → red error line, empty → muted 'No runs match these filters.', populated → table + pagination.

assertions.description: While the initial query is pending (data === undefined), a centered Loader2 spinner is shown in the runs card with py-16 vertical padding. No table header or empty-state text appears during loading.
assertions.precondition: The page has just mounted and no data has arrived yet

assertions.description: When the query completes with total=0 (either unfiltered-DB-empty or filter-matched-zero), the text 'No runs match these filters.' is rendered in muted color, centered, with py-10. The pagination controls and the table are not rendered in this state.
assertions.precondition: Query finished with zero rows (either no runs at all, or filter produced no matches)

assertions.description: If /api/vga/runs returns a non-2xx, the query throws an Error with message '<status> <statusText>: <detail>' and the page renders 'Failed to load runs: <err.message>' in text-red-500. The table and pagination are not rendered in this state.
assertions.precondition: The /api/vga/runs request returned a non-2xx response

assertions.description: At any moment the runs card shows exactly ONE of: spinner, error line, empty text, or table+pagination. Showing two simultaneously indicates a buggy conditional chain in the render tree.

groups.name: Pagination Controls
groups.description: When the table is populated, a footer row shows the current window and Prev/Next controls. The controls are disabled at the ends of the range. PAGE_SIZE is 50.

assertions.description: The footer shows a range string of the form '<start>–<end> of <total>' where start = (page-1)*50+1 and end = min(page*50, total). When the background query is refetching on a filter change (isFetching && !isLoading), the text is followed by an italic 'updating…' cue.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (populated state)

assertions.description: On page 1, the 'Prev' button (aria-label='Previous page') is disabled. The onClick handler uses Math.max(1, p-1) so a race-clicked Prev can never go below 1 even if the disabled state flickered.
assertions.precondition: page === 1 AND at least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: On the last page (page === Math.ceil(total/50)), the 'Next' button (aria-label='Next page') is disabled. The onClick handler uses Math.min(totalPages, p+1) for the same reason as Prev.
assertions.precondition: page === Math.ceil(total/50) AND at least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: Clicking Next advances state page by 1, sets ?page=N in the URL when N>1, and refetches /api/vga/runs with offset=(N-1)*50. The footer range string should update and the table rows should be the next 50 rows of the same query.
assertions.precondition: total > 50 AND at least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: Changing any filter (status, target_process, since, until) calls resetToFirstPage() so the user is not stranded on a page index that no longer has data under the new filter.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered and page > 1

groups.name: URL ↔ Filter State Sync
groups.description: The four filters plus the page index are mirrored into the URL query string. A shared link or bookmark must reproduce the exact filtered view. Sync is bidirectional via a single `lastSyncedRef` to prevent the state→URL effect from stomping params the user navigated in with.

assertions.description: On /vga/runs with no query string, the URL stays as '/vga/runs' (no trailing '?') and all filters are at defaults: status=All, target_process='', since='', until='', page=1.
assertions.precondition: The page is loaded with no query string

assertions.description: Navigating to /vga/runs?status=failed&target_process=notepad%2B%2B.exe&since=2026-04-10 seeds the filter inputs accordingly: status shows 'Failed', target input value is 'notepad++.exe' (decoded), since input value is '2026-04-10'. This works for BOTH client-side (router.push) and hard-reload navigation.
assertions.precondition: The page is navigated to with the specified query string

assertions.description: Editing any filter via the UI updates the URL via router.replace() to the minimal form (omitting status=all, empty strings, and page=1). Example: typing 'chrome.exe' in target and blurring should set ?target_process=chrome.exe (URL-encoded as needed), nothing else.

The URL↔state sync must NOT oscillate. The `lastSyncedRef` tracks the last-written URL query string; both the URL→state and state→URL effects compare the current string to the ref and early-exit on match. A buggy implementation causes visible URL flicker or silently strips params the user navigated in with — regression previously caught in manual testing on 2026-04-22.

groups.name: GET /api/vga/runs contract
groups.description: The page is backed by the Next.js route handler at /api/vga/runs which executes a single SELECT over runner.vga_runs LEFT JOIN runner.vga_state_machines. The contract is observable from the page: the table's column values come directly from the row shape, totals come from COUNT(*) OVER(), filter params shape the WHERE clause.

assertions.description: Each row rendered on the page maps 1:1 to a VgaRunListItem: { id, stateMachineId, stateMachineName, targetProcess, groundingModel, status, startedAt (ISO), endedAt (ISO|null) }. The groundingModel is fetched but NOT rendered by this page — /vga/runs does not expose it as a column; drop it only if memory/bandwidth matters.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: Rows are ordered by started_at DESC server-side (ORDER BY r.started_at DESC). The Started column, when read top-to-bottom, should be monotonically non-increasing. A row at index i with a startedAt earlier than index i+1 indicates the ORDER BY was dropped or overridden.
assertions.precondition: At least two run rows are rendered

assertions.description: The '<N> total' badge in the header and the 'of <N>' value in the pagination footer must agree and both must equal the COUNT(*) OVER() returned by the API. A disagreement between header and footer means the page is rendering two different totals (probably stale cache vs live response).
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (populated state)

assertions.description: Filtering by status applies `r.status = $?` server-side (exact match). Every row visible under a non-All status filter must have a status badge whose label corresponds to that filter value (status=failed → every badge says 'Failed').
assertions.precondition: A non-'All' status filter is active and at least one row is rendered

assertions.description: Filtering by target_process applies `sm.target_process = $?` server-side. The match is exact (case-sensitive). Every row visible under a target_process filter must have that exact value in its Target process cell; rows with different processes must not appear.
assertions.precondition: A target_process filter is active and at least one row is rendered

assertions.description: The API caps `limit` at 200 server-side, even if a client sends limit=1000. The page itself only sends limit=50 (PAGE_SIZE), but any integration consuming this route cannot use it to dump the whole table in one call.

groups.name: Visual Design & Typography
groups.description: Typography and color follow the theme tokens (bg-background, border-border, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground). The page header uses the same layout pattern as /vga to keep the two pages visually coherent.

assertions.description: The page heading 'VGA runs' is rendered as an <h1> with class 'text-lg font-semibold text-foreground'. It matches the /vga landing heading size exactly so switching between the two feels like one surface.
The header row has a bottom border (border-b border-border) separating it from the main scroll region. The border uses the theme's border token so it adapts to light/dark mode.

Started-at and Duration cells use 'text-xs text-muted-foreground' — they are smaller and dimmer than the primary columns (Run, State machine, Target, Status). This creates a two-tier visual hierarchy: identity columns prominent, metadata recedes.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: The Run-id cell uses a monospace font ('font-mono text-xs') so the hexadecimal 8-char prefix is easy to scan and visually diff.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

assertions.description: The empty-state text 'No runs match these filters.' must be readable against the card background. It uses text-muted-foreground which is intentionally subdued but must still pass WCAG AA on the card's bg token in both light and dark mode.

groups.name: Accessibility
groups.description: Semantic HTML (h1, table with th scope, labeled form controls), aria-labels on icon-only buttons, keyboard-focus affordances.

assertions.description: Each filter input/select has a <label htmlFor=...> pointing at it so screen readers announce the field name on focus: filter-status → 'Status', filter-target → 'Target process', filter-since → 'Since', filter-until → 'Until'.
Prev/Next buttons expose aria-label='Previous page'/'Next page' so the chevron icons aren't the only cue for AT users.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (populated state)

assertions.description: The back-arrow Link carries aria-label='Back to VGA landing' so the ArrowLeft icon isn't rendered as an unlabeled button to AT.
The run-id link in each row is a real <Link> (renders <a href>), so Tab-navigating through the table hits every row's detail link in source order.
assertions.precondition: At least one run row is rendered
